Ad Text:
Senior Systems Analyst - Solution Design & Requirements Lead

We are seeking a high-calibre Senior Systems Analyst to support complex ICT initiatives where strong business analysis, solution design and stakeholder engagement are critical to delivery success. This role will suit a proactive analyst who can translate business needs into clear technical requirements, produce quality functional specifications, assess feasibility and risk, and work closely with stakeholders to drive practical, well-documented outcomes across the full solution lifecycle.

Location: ACT / NSW
Contract Duration: 12-month contract with extension options, up to a maximum of 24 months total
Security Clearance Requirement: Due to the nature of this environment, candidates must hold a current Baseline clearance.

Essential Tasks:

Analyse business processes and requirements and translate them into clear technical and functional specifications
Develop solution designs and maintain high-quality solution and project documentation
Assess feasibility, effort, risks, benefits and impacts to support informed delivery decisions
Collaborate with stakeholders on testing, issue resolution, quality reviews and implementation activities
Key Criteria:

Strong experience leading requirements definition and management for complex initiatives
Proven capability in feasibility assessment, including business case, cost/benefit and risk analysis
Demonstrated expertise in systems design for large or complex environments
Strong stakeholder engagement, specialist advisory and documentation skills
How to Apply
Does this role sound perfect for you? If so, to register your interest in this role, please either contact Liam Murphy or the Compas Team on (02) 6230 0233 and send through your CV by following the link below.

Job Title: Manager, Portfolio Oversight, Fixed Income, Currency & Credit

Ad Text:
In this role, you’ll play a pivotal part in shaping how we price risk and make underwriting decisions across our home portfolio, working at the heart of our Pricing and Underwriting function.

This is a fixed-term contract with the opportunity to move into a permanent role at the end.

Day to day, you’ll apply actuarial principles to complex business challenges — analysing large data sets, building and refining pricing models, and translating insights into clear recommendations for senior stakeholders.

You’ll be hands-on with model development, performance monitoring and system implementation, while continuously identifying opportunities to improve accuracy, efficiency and portfolio outcomes in a fast-paced, data-driven environment.

Based in our Chermside office in Brisbane you’ll work in a hybrid model — in the office Tuesday to Thursday, with the flexibility to work from home on Monday and Friday.

You’ll love this role if you enjoy combining technical actuarial work with real business impact. This is more than just pricing and underwriting — you’ll help shape how we think about risk, performance and future growth, working alongside experienced actuarial professionals and gaining exposure to senior decision-makers.

What you’ll be doing

Supporting pricing and underwriting decisions through robust, accurate and timely data-driven analysis
Monitoring portfolio performance, identifying trends and investigating deviations from expected outcomes
Preparing and transforming internal and external data for modelling and technical review
Building and refining predictive models using actuarial techniques such as GLMs and machine learning methods (e.g. GBMs)
Translating model outputs into clear insights and recommending pricing adjustments to senior stakeholders
Designing and enhancing pricing frameworks and underwriting rules

What great looks like

Bachelor’s degree in Actuarial Science or a related quantitative field (e.g. mathematics, statistics)
Progress toward actuarial qualification
Strong knowledge of general insurance products and concepts
Experience building predictive models (e.g. GLMs, machine learning techniques)
Proficiency in tools such as Python, R, SQL, SAS or C#
Experience with data visualisation tools such as Power BI or Tableau

What you’ll get

Time off you can plan around
22 days annual leave, plus volunteer leave and annual salary reviews
A workplace you can feel
Food trucks and team celebrations that bring real energy to the day
Leaders who back your growth
Clear direction, regular coaching and real opportunities to step into new roles
Practical benefits that support you day to day
Free skin checks, flu jabs, wellbeing sessions, discounted gym access and discounted Youi insurance

Ready?

Apply and we’ll circle back.

To discover more about our culture, values, and what we have to offer, visit youi.com.au/about-us/careers or jump on Instagram @yourlifeatyoui to see what work life with us looks like.

 

Why Youi

We’re here to make insurance more human and provide a better alternative for our customers. As a challenger brand, we question the status quo to support people when life doesn’t go to plan, with real people helping real customers at the moments that matter most. It’s also a place where you can make your mark. We grow our own people here, with clear pathways, structured development and hands-on coaching helping many team members step into leadership roles across the business.

The role

As our Manager Technology Assurance, you’ll be at the forefront of ensuring that Auto & General’s technology systems and digital platforms operate safely, securely, and effectively. You will lead independent assurance reviews across our technology landscape, assessing the effectiveness of IT controls, risk management practices, and emerging technology initiatives. Partnering with a team of Assurance Analysts, you’ll provide strategic insights that shape technology risk decisions, strengthen compliance, and support senior leadership in safeguarding the organisation’s digital environment. This is a pivotal role where your expertise will directly influence the resilience and reliability of our technology systems.


Position Accountabilities

In this role, you will:

Lead Technology Assurance Programs: Oversee and deliver the annual assurance plan with a focus on technology systems, digital platforms, and emerging technologies, ensuring IT controls are robust and effective.
Drive Regulatory & Policy Compliance: Ensure technology operations comply with internal policies and external regulations (APRA, ASIC), collaborating closely with the Compliance and IT teams to resolve issues.
Provide Strategic Technology Insights: Deliver actionable assurance reports and recommendations on technology risk and control effectiveness, influencing senior leadership and committee decisions.
Foster Stakeholder Collaboration: Build strong relationships with executives, technology leaders, and key stakeholders to embed a proactive technology risk culture.
Champion Innovation in Assurance: Continuously improve technology assurance methodologies, leveraging new tools and techniques to enhance efficiency, coverage, and insight.
Lead and Develop the Assurance Team: Mentor and guide Assurance Analysts in conducting technology-focused reviews, control testing, and risk assessments, ensuring high-quality outcomes.
Drive Strategic Tech Initiatives: Lead cross-functional projects related to operational and technology risk, providing assurance input for digital transformation, IT programs, and emerging technology adoption.

 

What experience you’ll bring

Proven Technology Risk Expertise: 5+ years in risk, assurance, audit, or compliance, including 2–3 years in 2LOD technology assurance or IT risk.
Technical & Regulatory Knowledge: Strong understanding of IT controls, risk frameworks (COSO, ISO 31000), and regulations (APRA CPS 220/230/234, ASIC RG 271).
Analytical & Strategic Thinking: Ability to translate complex technology risks into clear, actionable insights.
Qualifications: Bachelor’s in Technology, Finance, Business, Risk, or Law; professional certifications (CISA, CIA, CRISC, CPA) highly desirable.
Stakeholder & Communication Skills: Able to influence and present confidently to executive-level stakeholders.
Leadership Skills: Experience leading and developing high-performing assurance or risk teams.
Demonstrated expertise: in designing, executing, and overseeing complex risk assessments, control testing, and assurance reviews. 
Thorough understanding: of the Technology Landscape, especially emerging technologies with a risk lens.

 

Location –Toowong

Close to a variety of public transport and easy parking options, the Toowong office has excellent end of trip facilities (including secure bike storage and showers), outdoor spaces and plenty of modern collaborative areas to work and connect with colleagues. 

Conveniently based at Toowong Village tower with views of the mountains and the Brisbane River, our location is only 4 km from the city and provides access to retail, restaurants and other amenities.

 

The benefits of working at A&G

Be rewarded - we recognise high performance and reward our people for their hard work through bonuses and other perks.
Work flexibility - with options to work from home up to two days per week.
Options for leave - life happens, so we’ve got volunteer days, an additional paid ‘ME’ day, paid parental leave and the opportunity to purchase additional leave to cover all of the big stuff.
Grow with us - we’ve got learning and professional development opportunities to suit everyone.
Give back - our A&G Difference program gives you the power to change our community for the better through volunteering, fundraising and donation opportunities for causes that you’re passionate about.
Take care of yourself - your wellbeing is important to us and our healthy mind and body hub, mental-health support and fitness discounts will help you be your best self.
Celebrate the wins - we love sharing our successes and celebrating together - join us and you’ve got a ticket to our many on-site events throughout the year, family fun days and annual celebrations.
Save money - as well as discounts on insurance products, we’ve teamed up with some incredible retailers, hospitality providers and others to bring you discounts on your purchases, no matter where you are in Australia.

 

About us

Auto & General (A&G) is the fastest-growing major Motor and Home insurer in Australia, providing insurance products and solutions to safeguard a brighter future for our customers and community.  

Our range of general insurance products including Car, Motorcycle, Home, Contents Pet and Travel products are delivered through our multi-award-winning brand Budget Direct and partnerships with leading brands - ING, Qantas, and Coles Insurance.

We’re excited about the future and we’re always on the lookout for talented, passionate individuals who can help us achieve our goal of being Australia’s best insurer! If this sounds like you, apply today.

 

Auto & General values individual differences and believes in fostering an inclusive culture that creates a great place to work for all. 


*A note from Auto & General to recruitment agencies: We politely ask that you avoid making any approaches or sending any unsolicited resumes to our Recruitment Team or Hiring Leaders across our business. Auto & General is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

About the Role

We’re seeking an experienced Test Analyst to support a major data transformation program within a highly regulated environment. This role sits within a critical initiative focused on modernising data platforms and ensuring the seamless continuity of reporting during a system transition.




You’ll play a key role in validating data loads and transformations between strategic data collection platforms and legacy data warehouses, ensuring accuracy, consistency, and reliability across the data ecosystem.




This is a hands‑on role suited to someone who is technically strong, highly detail‑oriented, and comfortable working closely with engineers and data specialists.




Key Responsibilities

Develop and execute test plans, test cases, and test scripts for data platform components, including ETL/ELT processes and data pipelines
Validate data migrations and transformations from legacy on‑premise systems (e.g. SQL Server, Oracle) to target data warehouses
Perform data reconciliation between source and target systems to ensure completeness, consistency, and accuracy
Execute data testing using SQL and/or Python to extract, compare, and validate large datasets
Investigate, document, and track defects using tools such as JIRA
Liaise with data engineers, architects, and delivery teams to support defect resolution and root cause analysis
Support User Acceptance Testing (UAT) by preparing data scenarios, guiding users, and capturing feedback
Ensure adherence to testing standards and contribute to the evolution of best‑practice testing approaches
Nature of the Work
Embedded within a large‑scale data transformation program
Hands‑on, technically focused testing across data pipelines and platforms
Strong collaboration with data engineers, architects, and senior technical specialists
Mix of:
Data validation and reconciliation
Defect investigation and reporting
Stakeholder collaboration across technology and data teams
What You’ll Bring
3+ years’ experience in a QA or data testing role, ideally within data migration or data platform projects
Strong SQL skills for data validation, reconciliation, and transformation testing
Solid understanding of ETL/ELT frameworks and data pipelines
Excellent attention to detail with strong problem‑solving capability
Clear written and verbal communication skills to engage cross‑functionally
Desirable Experience
Python or PySpark experience for automated data testing
Exposure to agile delivery, DevOps, or DataOps environments
Experience within financial services, regulatory reporting, or APRA collections

NV1 Clearance Required
Canberra, ACT
12 + 12 + 12 Month Contract

About Perilya Limited

Perilya Limited is a well-established Australian mining company with a proud history and a strong international footprint. Our operations include the iconic Broken Hill Operation in New South Wales - one of the world’s great base metals mines with over 130 years of continuous production, and Coridom in the Dominican Republic, the country’s first underground mining operation, which continues to deliver high-quality zinc, lead, copper, and silver.

Together, these assets form the foundation of a business with scale, resilience, and long-term growth opportunities.

Perilya is recognised for its commitment to operational excellence, safety leadership, and environmental responsibility. We invest in our people and communities, fostering diversity and creating genuine career pathways across both corporate and operational sites. Our collaborative culture values integrity, accountability, and continuous improvement, making Perilya a workplace where talented people can thrive and contribute to a sustainable future in mining.

The Role

We are seeking an experienced Business Analyst to join our Head Office Finance team in Adelaide. Reporting directly to the Finance Director, this role is central to supporting financial performance, strategic decision making and commercial outcomes across the business.

This is a highly finance-focused Business Analyst role with strong exposure to senior leadership. You will work at the intersection of finance, operations and strategy, providing insight-driven analysis to support business performance and investment decisions.

A key aspect of this role is partnering closely with our site operations teams. While based in Head Office, you will collaborate regularly and effectively with site-based stakeholders, including the ability to work remotely with operational teams to support informed, practical decision making.

Key responsibilities include:

Delivering detailed financial and commercial analysis to support executive decision making

Supporting budgeting, forecasting, long range planning and cost control processes

Developing and maintaining robust financial models, scenarios and sensitivities

Providing variance analysis, trend analysis and performance commentary

Supporting capital expenditure analysis, business cases and investment proposals

Enhancing management reporting, dashboards and financial metrics

Partnering closely with site operations and corporate stakeholders to improve financial and commercial outcomes

Collaborating effectively with site-based teams in a combination of remote and operationally focused environments

Driving continuous improvement in finance processes, systems and data quality

About You
You are a financially and commercially astute analyst with a strong finance foundation and the ability to influence outcomes through data, insight and collaboration with operational teams.

To succeed in this role, you will bring:

5–7 years’ experience in a Business Analyst, Commercial Analyst or Finance Analyst role

Strong background in financial analysis, modelling and reporting

Experience within mining, resources, manufacturing or capital‑intensive environments (highly regarded)

Advanced Excel skills and experience with ERP systems and BI/reporting tools (e.g. Power BI)

A solid understanding of budgeting, forecasting and financial controls

Strong stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to work collaboratively with site-based teams, including in remote or geographically dispersed environments

Confidence working with senior leaders and operational stakeholders alike

Tertiary qualification in Finance, Accounting, Economics or a related discipline

CA/CPA qualification (or progress toward) is desirable

Why Join Perilya?

Adelaide-based head office role with exposure to executive leadership

Opportunity to influence business performance in a critical industry

Collaborative, professional finance team environment

Long-term career development within an established mining company

How to Apply

To be considered for this role, please submit a brief covering letter and CV by following the APPLY NOW prompts.

At Perilya Limited, we value diversity and inclusion, strongly encouraging applications from women, Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, and candidates from culturally diverse backgrounds.

We are committed to building an inclusive workforce where everyone can contribute, grow, and succeed.

Job Title: Head of Finance- FP&A and Transformation
Ad Text:
We are seeking a commercially astute and technically strong finance leader to step into a unique and high-impact role that blends FP&A leadership with "hands on" data, BI and finance transformation ownership.

This is not a traditional Head of Finance role. You will lead the finance function while also driving a Finance AI and automation agenda, building a modern, data-driven capability that fundamentally reshapes how finance operates and supports the business.

About the Role:

Reporting to the CFO, this role sits at the intersection of finance, data and technology, with accountability across three core pillars:

FP&A Leadership- owning budgeting, forecasting, reporting and strategic financial insight
Finance Transformation- delivering automation, AI enablement and a single source of truth
Billing & Claims Optimisation- overseeing a shift to a near fully automated, exception-based model




You will lead a small but highly capable team and work closely with Technology, Operations and Executive Leadership to embed data-led decision making across the organisation.




Key Responsibilities:

FP&A & Commercial Insight

Own the full FP&A function including budgets, forecasts and long range planning
Deliver high quality month-end reporting, variance analysis and performance insights
Partner with senior stakeholders to support strategic decisions and growth initiatives
Lead financial modelling, scenario analysis and M&A support




Finance AI & Transformation

Drive the implementation of finance automation and AI-enabled processes
Build and own a single source of truth through scalable dashboards and data models
Replace manual, spreadsheet driven processes with automated, system led solutions
Define data governance, structures and reporting standards




Data, BI & Systems Leadership

Work "hands on" with SQL, Python and BI tools to build reporting and analytics capability
Lead the design of dashboards, data pipelines and reporting frameworks
Collaborate with technology teams on system integration and data architecture




About You

You are a rare blend of finance leader and "hands on" data practitioner, comfortable moving between strategic thinking and technical execution.

10+ years in FP&A or commercial finance
Strong experience in financial modelling, reporting and business partnering
Capability in SQL or Python or BI tools (Power BI, Tableau or similar)
Proven track record in finance transformation, automation or systems uplift
Experience designing data models, dashboards and reporting frameworks
Strong stakeholder engagement skills, including executive and board level communication
Experience in M&A, or exposure to high growth / transformation environments (desirable)




Why This Role Stands Out

Opportunity to build a modern finance function from the ground up
High visibility and impact across executive leadership
"Hands on" influence over data, systems and strategic direction
Lead a shift to exception-based, automated finance operations




What Success Looks Like

A fully embedded FP&A cadence delivering clear, actionable insight
Finance processes significantly automated and scalable
A trusted single source of truth for financial and operational reporting
Finance recognised as a strategic partner to the business
Measurable improvements in efficiency, accuracy and decision-making capability




You must hold full, unrestricted working rights in Australia on a permanent basis.

Job Title: Senior Data & Insights Analyst (12 m contract)
Ad Text:
About the Opportunity

As Flight Centre Travel Group’s (FCTG) Senior Data & Insights Analyst you will drive how data is leveraged in solving business problems within the dynamic Leisure travel industry. You will consult with stakeholders in problem-definition, setting success metrics and shaping the way forward through data insights, analytics and effective communication with your audience.

You will use your deep practical understanding of the data, workflows, methods and tooling to autonomously drive analysis and reporting within your scope. You can adapt your tooling and approach based on business and user needs.

This involves reporting, visualisation and taking ownership of workflows and structures to make solutions scalable, reproducible, and long-term focused.

You will also collaborate with peers in data engineering, insights, analytics & data science and AI, supporting initiatives for data quality and stability, using your experience to think of scalable solutions.

This full time Brisbane-based role is for an initial 12-month contract to cover parental leave.

 

What you will do:

Extract data from multiple data sources and combine them into the required datasets for model building or analytics
Conduct exploratory data profiling techniques, product crosstabs, common summary descriptive statistics, etc.
Pick analytically valid approaches, appropriate in terms of level of effort, favouring iterative delivery that solve for the objective, not the ask
Demonstrate understanding of business domain and can recommend model changes, continuously evolving information and requirements
Become knowledgeable in visualisation tools (Power BI), creating complex calculations using advanced features
Clearly and confidently articulate decision-making rationale, solutions, methodologies and frameworks to team members and both technical and non-technical partners
Leverage data to shape your product squad's strategy, providing support and/or leadership wherever needed
Play a critical role in attacking opaque, unstructured problems; develop core analytic frameworks that shape the way the company thinks about our business
Own projects that span disparate groups with varying levels of technical fluency from end-to-end
Build out data availability and business intelligence solutions for your team
Regularly present high-level narrative-driven analyses to all levels of management across the organisation
Build a data-driven culture wherever you turn, from working with PMs on KPIs, to partnering with engineers on data models, to making sure that all decisions accurately utilise the most powerful data available
Mentor data team members in articulating ideas and viewpoints, managing resources, and driving the resolution of issues
Perform exploratory analyses and in-depth research on large data sets to uncover, size and advocate for opportunities that improve the customer experience and key growth KPIs like customer conversion and retention
Communicate not just findings, but also your recommendations to key stakeholders and leadership - you will use your insights to advocate for our customers
Partner closely with stakeholders across various teams to ensure that priorities and resources are aligned

 

What you will need:

5+ years of experience in product and customer analytics
3+ years of full-time working experience in quantitative analysis roles
Experience using relational databases and big data via writing performant queries in SQL against multi-terabyte and billion row datasets in Azure Data Lake, EDW, AWS or Hadoop environments
Experience with modern web analytics tools (Adobe analytics, Google Analytics, Mixpanel, etc.)
Experience using modern visualisation tools (Power BI, Tableau, Looker, Qlik)
Solid experience hiring, building, mentoring and leading a team
Experience in a relevant area – travel, E-commerce, or technology is a strong plus
Excellent oral and written skills to communicate complex issues and influence others internally and externally

Applications Portfolio Owner (Technical Product Owner) 
Sydney, Hybrid | Permanent | Salary Circa $200,000 package + performance bonus + employee perks

The Company
Our client is one of the most iconic brands in the country and runs one of the most complex technology environments in the Australian market. Thousands of staff depend on their platforms daily, spanning everything from customer experience through to safety-critical operational systems that keep the business moving. The technology function is mature, well-invested and operating at genuine enterprise scale.

The Opportunity
We are hiring across multiple domains. Each role will see you take full end-to-end accountability for a defined suite of enterprise applications aligned to a specific business capability. Portfolios span digital, commercial, operational and enterprise service domains.

This is a senior portfolio ownership role, not a squad-level PO position or a project delivery role. Day to day you will be driving roadmap strategy, overseeing project delivery, managing vendors and budgets, owning BAU stability, and engaging stakeholders from analyst level through to executive and CTO visibility.

You will also be expected to build genuine relationships with business partners and end users to understand real problems and drive meaningful outcomes.

What You'll Own

Portfolio strategy, roadmap and business case development
Delivery oversight across enhancements, upgrades and transformation initiatives
Vendor selection, commercial management and performance accountability
BAU stability, risk management and service performance
Budget and financial governance across your portfolio
Stakeholder engagement from analyst level through to executive and CTO level
Leadership and coaching of a small team (1-2 people)
What We're Looking For

5+ years in a technical product, application management or delivery role within a large, complex enterprise
Genuine end-to-end ownership experience across a portfolio of enterprise applications
Experience across digital and operational applications, not just finance or back-office systems
Strong vendor and commercial management capability
Financially literate with budget governance experience
A communicator who can operate credibly from delivery teams through to executive level
Strong business partnering skills, this role interfaces closely with senior business stakeholders
Analytical, pragmatic, and able to shift between strategic and tactical as needed
CRM or platform experience (Salesforce etc.) advantageous but not essential
The Details

Location: Sydney, hybrid (minimum three days per week onsite)
Engagement: Permanent
Salary: Circa $200,000 package + performance bonus + employee perks
Applications: Please apply with a current Word version of your CV
When applying, please ensure your CV clearly highlights:

The applications or platforms you have owned, including your involvement in developing and managing product roadmaps
Your experience running RFPs and managing vendor relationships (What was the RFP for?)
Any exposure to working with procurement, legal, risk or data teams
Cross-functional teams you have managed or coordinated
Examples of direct engagement with business stakeholders and end users to inform product or platform decisions
If you have owned a technology portfolio in a complex enterprise and want a role with real accountability and scope, we want to hear from you.

We’ve been proudly serving Australian communities since 1914 and have grown to become a top 30-listed company on the ASX with a portfolio of iconic homegrown brands. At the heart of our continued success is over 115,000 team members working across the country, all contributing to bringing our customers smiles. You’ll not only get to see your work make a difference, you’ll be part of a team working together to help Australians eat and live better every day.

 

About the team

 

Our Technology team is the backbone of the business, building and maintaining the information technology infrastructure that support Coles Group. As a part of the Technology team, you get the opportunity to work through complex, challenging, meaningful problems using large data sets and new technologies to expand your skills and experience.



About the Role

 

We’re looking for a Senior Product Analyst to join a growing product team and play a key role in shaping how our retail operations evolve.

 

This is a newly created position focused on leading discovery, improving how requirements are defined, and ensuring strong alignment between operations and technology. You’ll deep dive into store processes and existing tools, identify opportunities for improvement, and support major initiatives including digital ticketing, future inventory, and stock management platforms.

 

Working closely with Product, Engineering, and business stakeholders, you’ll help drive better decisions, clearer requirements, and more effective outcomes.

 

Key Responsibilities

 

Lead discovery and analysis across business processes and systems
Elicit and document functional and non-functional requirements
Write clear user stories and acceptance criteria
Map processes, data flows, and system interactions
Identify inefficiencies and recommend improvements
Partner with Product, Engineering, and stakeholders to shape solutions
Support delivery across Agile and project-based work
Identify risks and support testing and validation
Coach and uplift analysis standards across the team

 

About You & Your Skills

 

Experience as a Senior Business Analyst or Product Analyst in complex environments
Strong ability to translate between business and technical teams
Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills
Strong analytical and problem-solving capability
Experience across discovery, requirements, and delivery
Proactive, hands-on approach with a focus on outcomes
Experience with Jira and Confluence

 

Nice to have:

 

Retail experience
Transformation program experience
Exposure to enterprise systems

 



What’s in it for you?

 

Flexible working options: We know that work is only one part of your life, so we actively encourage a positive work-life balance and provide hybrid working options to help you achieve it.
Office perks: Take advantage of our gym facility and fitness classes, free parking, BBQ area, mini-Coles supermarket, fooderie hub where you can sample new products before they hit the shelves, school holiday program and so much more when you come in.
Discounts: Eligible team members receive 5% discount all year round on your Supermarket and Liquor online and in-store purchases. We also offer additional periods of double discount (10%) at various times throughout the year. As well as hotdeals exclusive to team members that translate into additional savings.
Reward through recognition: Give and receive recognition, linked to our Coles values, through our digital recognition platform ‘mythanks’. You can accumulate points to redeem in the online shop for exciting gifts and electronic gift cards from an extensive range of retailers.
Opportunities for learning and development: No matter where you start within our diverse business, you’ll have experiences, exposure, and education to satisfy you. Discover and explore a variety of career development programs and job-specific training.
Paid parental leave: We understand how important your life outside work is and offer permanent team members paid parental leave to support you in balancing work and family.
Investment in your future: Our annual team member share plan offer allows eligible team members to make regular pre-tax salary sacrifice deductions to purchase Coles Group shares.

 

About the recruitment process

 

We’re continuing to build a gender equitable team, and a culture that is just as diverse, inclusive and welcoming as the communities we serve. We are committed to creating a workplace that is safe and respectful for our team. We encourage applications from people of all ages, cultures (including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples), abilities, sexual orientation and gender identities.

 

We’re happy to adjust our recruitment process to support candidates with disability. For further information and additional contact details visit the ‘Our Recruitment Process’ section of our careers site or email inclusionrecruitment@coles.com.au

Job Title: Consultant / Senior Consultant - Corporate Finance / Advisory (Healthcare)
Category: Corporate Finance & Investment Banking (Banking & Financial Services)
Ad Text:
About Paxon Consulting Group

Paxon is a leading advisory firm providing financial, commercial and strategic advice to support clients in making critical business decisions – specialising in the design and delivery of innovative commercial and financial solutions for major infrastructure and service redesign projects across the Asia Pacific region and the Middle East.

Our people are innovative, creative and experts in delivering advice that drives value for our clients, for the sectors in which they operate, and for the broader economy.

Paxon’s dedicated health-sector team works closely with senior leaders and decision-makers in public and private healthcare organisations at every level of the healthcare ecosystem, across Australia and in the Middle East. Each year Paxon advises on over $5 billion of new hospital infrastructure, influences the development of models of care and the way that innovation and technology can assist.  We also advise on specialist hospital services including pharmacy, medical imaging, rehabilitation, chemotherapy and dialysis.

We provide robust analysis and decision-making support, to help our clients make critical decisions about how healthcare is delivered. We often operate at the intersection of public and private sectors, helping our clients to create an effective, value-driven healthcare system.

The types of strategic decisions we typically support clients with include:

·          Business case and feasibility studies for health care programs, projects and assets

·          Financing for infrastructure investments

·          Mergers and acquisition / divestment of hospital and health assets

·          Collaborative partnerships between Governments and the private sector

·          Service outsourcing and procurement

·          Service reconfiguration and transitions

·          Costing and modelling of new business models

·          New model of care development

·          Evaluation and redesign of health programs

Beyond healthcare, Paxon has market-leading expertise in many other social infrastructure sectors, including education, housing, real estate, urban renewal and land activation, corrections, sport and recreation, renewable energy, water, ports and airports. Paxon’s core strengths are in financial, transaction and PPP (public private partnerships) advisory for social infrastructure projects.  We’ve advised on over US$130 billion of projects over the last 20 years.

The Role

Due to continued growth, Paxon is seeking Consultants or Senior Consultants to join our team in both our Perth and Sydney offices. This is the ideal next step for someone with prior experience in financial modelling in an advisory field, who is wanting to retain their technical skills, but progress further into commercial and financial advisory support.

As part of our team, you will have the opportunity to engage with healthcare clients and stakeholders across Australia, Asia Pacific and the Middle East, providing expert advice and leadership in various aspects of health system development and financing, including:

Collaborate closely with clients to understand their specific objectives and challenges

Undertake research, financial modelling and risk assessment for a range of healthcare infrastructure and service redesign projects

Commercial structuring of projects and healthcare investments, including development of commercial and financial structures and contracting arrangements

Assess the financial, operational and strategic dimensions of projects and policies

Prepare clear, compelling reports, recommendations and presentations for clients and stakeholders

The financial and commercial analysis of the impact and effectiveness of various healthcare projects, facilitating informed decision-making processes

This is the ideal role for you if you:

·         Have several years’ relevant experience in advisory, banking or corporate organisations and have a good grounding that has honed your commercial thinking, communication and technical skills

·         Have since realised that you want to work in Project or Corporate Finance in an advisory capacity and accelerate your career by working on high-profile, meaningful projects

·         Have experience in the health care sector

·         Have a finance / commerce background backed by studies in mathematics, data science, engineering or finance

·         Are proud to be known for your excellent Excel skills, commercial acumen and exceptional communication and presentation skills

·         Enjoy solving complex problems effectively through the use of your research and analytical skills

·         Are prepared to take ownership for your work and professional development and ask questions, rather than waiting for the answers to be given to you

·         Are looking for somewhere that you can grow your career over the long-term whilst working with smart people on interesting projects that have a social purpose.

Applications

This is a great opportunity to join a team embarking on an exciting journey of growth, contributing to leading social infrastructure projects that will positively impact millions of people.

If you possess the skills and experience outlined above, we encourage you to apply by submitting your resume and cover letter.

Company: Queensland Rural and Industry Development Authority (QRIDA)
Category:
Management (Information & Communication Technology)
Government - State (Government & Defence)
Ad Text:
The Senior Manager, Enterprise Applications, is responsible for the strategic direction, development, and governance of QRIDA's core software assets. You will lead a high-performing team to build, test, and release digital solutions that enable QRIDA's financial services, ensuring all software changes are technically sound and successfully adopted by the business.

As the Senior Manager, Enterprise Applications, you will lead the software development team to build and maintain high-performing enterprise applications using modern architectural patterns.

Key responsibilities include:

Define the technical standards, tools, and methodologies (e.g., Agile, Scrum) used across the development lifecycle.

Establish and govern a robust organisational release strategy, including automated QA, performance, and security testing.

Oversee the release functions to ensure that all software meets defined "Definition of Done" (DoD) criteria and security benchmarks before release.

Lead the Digital Change function to manage the human impact of technology transitions, including stakeholder communication, training, and business readiness.

Manage the enterprise application budget and lifecycle, addressing technical debt and software renewals.

Work with the Senior Manager Analytics and Reporting to oversee the integration of Data Engineering and Machine Learning capabilities into user-facing applications.

For a full description of the key responsibilities, please refer to the role description.

Joining our passionate team means an opportunity to learn, further your career, have your ideas heard and make a difference for rural and regional Queenslanders every day.

Staff have access to:

Salary Packaging, 12.75% super and leave loading

Flexible working arrangements including balancing of in office/working from home arrangements

Career and Performance Development Planning with the focus on setting of goals and career development

Learning & Development including access to the QRIDA Aspire Scheme

Employee assistance and benefits programs including access to Rewards Gateway (after meeting eligibility requirements)

QRIDA is a small organisation of just over two hundred employees. We value diversity in our community and work hard to foster an environment of inclusivity and trust.

The Queensland Rural and Industry Development Authority (QRIDA) is a specialist provider of government financial and advisory support to rural and regional Queensland.

As a statutory body of the Queensland Government we have been helping primary producers and industry recover from natural disaster, improve sustainability and profitability, make a start in business, and overcome difficult conditions, for over 30 years.

As the Queensland Government's experts in rural finance, we also administer the State Government's Farm Business Debt Mediation program, the Farm Debt Reconstruction Office, conduct a biennial Rural Debt Survey, undertake research and provide advice to the Minister for Agricultural Industry Development and Fisheries and Minister for Rural Communities.

QRIDA is empowered under the Rural and Regional Adjustment Act 1994 to deliver additional programs and services across a range of industry sectors and to local, state and territory governments throughout Australia and for the Australian Government.

We're committed to do more to be a proactive, responsible and reliable provider of financial management services and assistance to create sustainable rural communities.

To see what we are working on now visit our website at www.qrida.qld.gov.au

Senior FP&A Leadership Opportunity | Multinational  
Support Strategic Growth & Drive Performance Outcomes
12 month Fixed Term Contract with view to permanency
The Opportunity
  
An established and evolving multinationanal organisation is seeking a Group FP&A Manager in a 12 month fixed term contract, with the view for permanency. Reporting to the CFO, this position will join the business in a key leadership capacity and act as a senior finance business partner across multiple functions, driving performance through planning, insight, and analysis.
  
This is a high-impact opportunity to support the business during a period of strategic growth and transformation. You’ll play a lead role in performance analysis, budgeting, forecasting and scenario planning — combining strong financial acumen with data-driven insights to influence key commercial decisions.
  
The Challenge
  
As Senior FP&A Manager, your broad remit will include:

Managing and executing the planning and budgeting process across key segments, including the annual budget cycle and quarterly forecast cadence
Supporting and managing the development and enhancement of the mid-term forecasting tool, with a focus on value drivers and long-range ambition.
Developing and managing performance reporting frameworks, including KPIs and key metrics
Coordinating monthly performance reviews and supporting strategic insight for executive stakeholders
Delivering board-level reporting 
Analysing large and complex data sets, leveraging BI tools to generate commercial insights
Partnering with corporate and operational teams to improve analysis and accountability
Supporting scenario modelling, investment analysis, and strategic planning projects
Leading initiatives to improve reporting processes, tools, and data capability
Mentoring junior team members and fostering a culture of continuous improvement and collaboration
The Expertise
  
To be successful in this critical role, you will bring the following:

Experience working in a large, complex organisation, ideally ASX listed, with corporate exposure.
Strong background in financial planning, budgeting, forecasting and analytics
Strong data analytics capability and experience working with BI tools such as Power BI and TM1
Strong financial acumen, with a deep understanding of forecasting methodologies and value driver models.
Proven ability to influence and partner with senior stakeholders across both Finance and Operations.
CA or CPA qualification with relevant tertiary qualifications in Finance, Accounting, or Business
The Next Step
  
If this position will enhance your career and you would like further details on the client, the role, remuneration or a copy of the position description, please email matt@thompsoncook.com.au
Or alternatively please apply per the link below.

We recruit for key finance function roles, including Accounts Payable, Accounts Receivable, Payroll, Financial Accountants, Management Accountants, Finance Managers, Financial Controllers, and Chief Financial Officers, as well as positions in Treasury, Tax, and Audit. We support our clients with both permanent and contracting recruitment solutions.

About us:

At Lockheed Martin Australia, we believe that when people are empowered, inspired, and supported, they can achieve extraordinary things. We embrace flexibility, so our people can be their best inside and outside of work. We champion inclusion and continuous learning, ensuring all voices are heard and valued. And we thrive on collaboration, turning challenges into opportunities for growth and innovation.  As a leader in national security, our innovative and diverse programs create a more secure future for Australia across space, air, land, sea, and cyber domains. Every day is a chance to solve the world’s most complex challenges and make a profound impact. Experience it here.

Delivery. Assurance. Real Outcomes

We are seeking an experienced program delivery leader to join our Program Excellence function as Senior Manager, Program Excellence (Australia).

This is not a traditional governance and compliance role. It is a hands-on leadership position focused on improving how programs deliver — strengthening mobilisation, surfacing risks early, uplifting capability and ensuring programs remain predictable, controlled and outcome-focused. You will play a key role in transitioning our Program Performance governance led model to an outcomes focused Program Excellence approach.

This role will operate at the centre of complex programs, working alongside Business Area leaders, Program Directors, PMs and other Functional Leads to turn insight into action and improve delivery performance at scale.

 About the Role

You will lead the operational delivery of Program Excellence services across Australia — combining assurance, advisory support and capability uplift.

This role works across multiple programs and business areas, both domestically and internationally, to:

Ensure programs are set up for success from day one

Provide early visibility of delivery risks and issues

Strengthen governance, planning and performance discipline

Support teams to recover quickly when programs drift

You will be expected to engage directly with programs — not just review them.

 About You:

You are a strategic and hands-on leader with strong experience in program delivery, governance, or assurance within complex environments. You bring:

Deep understanding of program management frameworks and governance practices - you understand what good looks like because you’ve delivered it.

The ability to translate data into clear, actionable insights

Ability to identify issues early and intervene effectively in a collaborative manner

A passion for continuous improvement and capability uplift – comfortable leading change and influencing behaviours

Strong stakeholder engagement and influencing skills at all levels

A pragmatic, commercially aware mindset focused on delivery results

Experience working within regulated environments (highly regarded)

Experience in uplifting capability and improving how teams deliver

 Why This Role Matters

As our programs grow in scale and complexity, we are evolving from a traditional Program Performance model to a Program Excellence approach. You have an opportunity to contribute to the shaping and building of a stronger, more predictable delivery culture.

This role is central to that shift — helping us:

Move from compliance to real delivery performance

Provide a whole-of-program / portfolio view across risk, governance and execution

Enable data-driven decision making through consistent tools and insight

Strengthen how we deliver across a OneLMA operating model

How we reward and support you:

When you join Lockheed Martin, you work on projects you won’t experience anywhere else.

We offer you a range of benefits built to suit you, no matter what stage of life you are in. 

Flexible work & Hybrid work options

Access to our online discounts program offering immediate discounts or cashback

A variety of leave options including Purchase Leave to support you and your family  

Additional benefits such as Novated Leasing and Fitness Programs

Learning & Development platforms to encourage ongoing self-development

Special Requirements:

Applicants must be eligible to obtain and maintain an Australian Defence Security Clearance and employment may be contingent on the provision of further personal information including citizenship to ensure compliance with international export control legislation and regulations.

Equal Opportunity Statement:

Lockheed Martin Australia supports Equal Employment Opportunity and values diversity in the workplace by creating a place of employment that is, free of unlawful discrimination and is harassment free, fair, ethical, inclusive, and values the contribution of all its employees. By leveraging our employees’ unique talents and experiences, we will deliver innovative, affordable solutions and unparalleled customer value.

As part of our endeavours to increase participation of Indigenous Australians, we welcome applications from all people, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people.

We are committed to creating a workplace where everyone can thrive. If you are selected for this role, we would be happy to discuss any accommodations that will help you to be successful.

We are seeking an experienced program delivery leader to join our Program Excellence function as Senior Manager, Program Excellence (Australia).

This is not a traditional governance and compliance role. It is a hands-on leadership position focused on improving how programs deliver — strengthening mobilisation, surfacing risks early, uplifting capability and ensuring programs remain predictable, controlled and outcome-focused. You will play a key role in transitioning our Program Performance governance led model to an outcomes focused Program Excellence approach.

This role will operate at the centre of complex programs, working alongside Business Area leaders, Program Directors, PMs and other Functional Leads to turn insight into action and improve delivery performance at scale.

 About the Role

You will lead the operational delivery of Program Excellence services across Australia — combining assurance, advisory support and capability uplift.

This role works across multiple programs and business areas, both domestically and internationally, to:

Ensure programs are set up for success from day one

Provide early visibility of delivery risks and issues

Strengthen governance, planning and performance discipline

Support teams to recover quickly when programs drift

You will be expected to engage directly with programs — not just review them.

 About You:

You are a strategic and hands-on leader with strong experience in program delivery, governance, or assurance within complex environments. You bring:

Deep understanding of program management frameworks and governance practices - you understand what good looks like because you’ve delivered it.

The ability to translate data into clear, actionable insights

Ability to identify issues early and intervene effectively in a collaborative manner

A passion for continuous improvement and capability uplift – comfortable leading change and influencing behaviours

Strong stakeholder engagement and influencing skills at all levels

A pragmatic, commercially aware mindset focused on delivery results

Experience working within regulated environments (highly regarded)

Experience in uplifting capability and improving how teams deliver

This role is central to that shift — helping us:

Move from compliance to real delivery performance

Provide a whole-of-program / portfolio view across risk, governance and execution

The Role
Are you an experienced and motivated consultant with a passion for helping customers get real value from Microsoft 365 Copilot and AI agents? Do you excel at advising customers through uncertainty, guiding them to the right solutions, and building alongside them? If so, we invite you to apply for this exciting opportunity to join our highly agile, collaborative, and skilled Delivery Team.

Your expertise will help customers move forward with confidence through iterative, consultative engagements, shaping ongoing programs of work that deliver measurable outcomes with M365 Copilot, Agent Builder and Copilot Studio.

Key Responsibilities

Partner with customers to understand their business, identify where Copilot and agents can deliver real value, and advise on what can and should be done.
Guide customers through change, bringing a consultative point of view on AI adoption, use case prioritisation, responsible AI, and the evolving Copilot ecosystem.
Design and hands-on build AI agents using low-code tools including Copilot Studio and Agent Builder, iterating quickly with customers to refine and improve solutions.
Lead enablement activities including training sessions, masterclasses, hands-on workshops, co-building sessions and coaching, helping customer teams become self-sufficient with M365 Copilot, Agent Builder and Copilot Studio.
Run iterative engagements and ongoing programs of work, supporting customers on their Copilot journey.
Collaborate with technical team members on integrations, data sources, governance and extensions needed to support robust agent solutions.
Create clear, high-quality customer deliverables including solution designs, adoption plans, enablement materials and value-realisation artefacts.
Build and nurture relationships with key stakeholders, from end users to executive sponsors, throughout each engagement.
Offer subject matter expertise on M365 Copilot, Copilot Studio, Agent Builder and the broader Microsoft AI platform, and stay current as the technology evolves at pace.
Troubleshoot and resolve functional issues in Copilot and agent solutions, working with technical team members as needed.
Collaborate with engagement and program leads to ensure timely delivery, good use of investment, and strong customer outcomes.

Skills & Experience Required

Proven experience as a functional consultant on the Microsoft platform, with a track record of advising customers and delivering high-value solutions.
Hands-on experience building with Microsoft Copilot Studio and Agent Builder, or demonstrable low-code / Power Platform experience and a strong appetite to specialise in agents.
Strong working knowledge of Microsoft 365 Copilot and how it is applied across business scenarios.
A consultative mindset, able to advise, explain clearly, enable customers and guide them to the right outcome.
A genuine curiosity for AI and the ability to keep pace with a rapidly evolving technology landscape, continually learning and bringing new thinking to customers.
Strong understanding of business processes, enabling you to translate ambiguous business needs into practical agent and Copilot solutions.
Demonstrable advisory or consultancy experience, with a track record of delivering strategic guidance and helping customers realise value.
Excellent communication and stakeholder management abilities, comfortable engaging at all levels from end users to executives.
Confidence running training sessions, masterclasses, hands-on workshops, co-build sessions and coaching - helping others learn by doing.
Exceptional problem solving and analytical skills, adept at framing ambiguous challenges and applying AI technology thoughtfully to solve them.
Comfortable delivering under iterative, outcome-based approaches, including ongoing programs of work.
Awareness of responsible AI, governance, security and data considerations relevant to Copilot and agent solutions.
Ability to work independently and as part of a team, balancing multiple customer engagements at once.

If you are a skilled functional consultant with great communication skills, a passion for Microsoft Copilot and AI agents, and a talent for guiding customers through change, we encourage you to apply.

IT Audit & Compliance Analyst

Company: BGIS
Employment Type: Full Time (12 Month Contract)
Location: Open to applicants across Australia

About the Role

We are seeking a motivated IT Audit & Compliance Analyst to support and strengthen our IT General Controls (ITGC) and Sarbanes-Oxley (SOX) compliance program across APAC. This is a great opportunity to build your expertise in cybersecurity, information security frameworks, and risk management within a dynamic, multi-sector environment.

In this role, you will help monitor control effectiveness, support audit activities, and drive continuous improvement in our compliance posture. You will also work closely with senior leadership, external auditors, and cross-functional teams, making strong stakeholder management and communication skills essential.

Key Responsibilities

Controls Testing, Documentation & Compliance Management

Support the IT Risk & Controls Manager in monitoring compliance with internal IT policies and external regulatory requirements, including SOX and ISO 27001
Assist in the execution and documentation of IT control testing procedures to evaluate control design and effectiveness, identifying potential gaps
Perform control testing in line with established methodologies and frameworks such as SOX ITGC and ISO 27001
Maintain accurate, well-organised documentation of testing activities, results, and supporting evidence to ensure audit readiness
Assist in preparing audit observations, control deficiencies, and risk findings for internal review prior to stakeholder communication
Support the development of corrective and preventive action plans aligned with business objectives
Track remediation actions from internal and external audits, following up with control owners to ensure timely closure and evidence collection
Support audit engagements by collating and providing audit evidence to internal and external auditors in a timely manner
Provide guidance to control owners on execution requirements, documentation standards, and testing expectations
Identify opportunities to improve audit efficiency and control testing processes
Contribute to IT risk register maintenance and compliance reporting metrics
Stay informed on regulatory requirements, industry standards, and audit practices
What We're Looking For

We are looking for candidates who bring both strong technical capability in SOX/ITGC environments and the ability to operate confidently in stakeholder-facing situations. Many applicants typically demonstrate one of these strengths, but success in this role requires both.

Essential Experience

About the Team

As a member of our diverse and skilled ANZ Solution Consulting team, you will have the opportunity to communicate and demonstrate the power of Workday's applications to prospects and customers alike. It is this team's job to bring the value of Workday solutions to life and showcase how the world of work can be transformed. Along with working closely with our sales counterparts, this team also sits at the exciting intersection of our Product Management, Consulting Services, Marketing, and Value Management teams, providing exposure to various areas of the business.

About the Role

We have an exciting opportunity for a Financials Solution Consultant for our ANZ team! Our team demonstrates the value of next generation SaaS Financial Management Applications and Artificial Intelligence Agents to Workday prospects and customers. Through interactive pre-sales discovery meetings, and by leveraging deep Financial Accounting skills and complex Enterprise Software product knowledge, Workday Solution Consultants provide Financials expertise, Executive presentations, and Workday Financials product demonstrations, in partnership with our sales team, to customers throughout the sales cycle. Our team works in a highly complex environment that requires curiosity, flexibility, and drive.  Workday is looking for a Solution Consultant with Financials & ERP expertise to join ANZ Solution Consultant team working across medium and larger enterprise customers. 

About You

Basic Qualifications

Experience across finance and accounting domains, including  GL, AR, AP, Fixed Assets, Multi-Company and Consolidations, Multi-Currency
5+ years of functional, implementation, consulting, or presales experience with Financial Management or ERP solutions.
Strong understanding of additional Finance-related functions such as Procurement, Project Costing is beneficial
Proven experience engaging with enterprise finance stakeholders, including Controllers, CFOs, and Finance Transformation leaders.
Exposure to Financial Management solutions from Workday, Oracle, SAP, Microsoft or similar would be an advantage
Experience in engagements with enterprises in Australia and New Zealand across one or more of the following industries - FSI, Higher Education, Healthcare, Government and Public Services, Retail, Professional Services etc.

Other Qualifications / Profile

You are a credible, dynamic finance transformation expert, ready to help organizations sell and operate the future of finance today.
An outstanding and confident presenter with strong communication and influencing skills, capable of engaging and persuading CFOs, Finance VPs, Controllers, and senior IT leaders.
Extensive experience in the finance and ERP technology space, with deep knowledge of Financial Management, ERP, EPM, and analytics platforms, and how they are applied to modernize finance operations.
Strong passion for AI, machine learning, automation, and agentic AI, with a clear point of view on how these technologies are transforming finance processes, insights, and decision-making.
Genuinely excited about Workday Financial Management and motivated to represent Workday’s next-generation AI-powered finance capabilities to the market.
Deep understanding of the finance operating model, enabling you to speak fluently with the Office of the CFO about governance, controls, efficiency, agility, and business outcomes—not just system features.
Proven ability to map customer-specific finance challenges directly to technology and AI driven solutions, articulating value in terms of faster close, improved visibility, stronger compliance, and reduced manual effort—rather than simply listing product functionality.

Our Approach to Flexible Work
 

With Flex Work, we’re combining the best of both worlds: in-person time and remote. Our approach enables our teams to deepen connections, maintain a strong community, and do their best work. We know that flexibility can take shape in many ways, so rather than a number of required days in-office each week, we simply spend at least half (50%) of our time each quarter in the office or in the field with our customers, prospects, and partners (depending on role). This means you'll have the freedom to create a flexible schedule that caters to your business, team, and personal needs, while being intentional to make the most of time spent together. Those in our remote "home office" roles also have the opportunity to come together in our offices for important moments that matter.
